weather_report:
A clear night has folded over Subiaco, the sky rinsed clean and steady, with the last warmth of day already a memory. Forecasts keep the heavens open and uncomplicated into the small hours, so darkness feels spacious rather than cold. There’s no sun now and the UV index is essentially asleep; clarity is the story, a simple, glassy vault above. If this calm holds through dawn, the first light should arrive bright and unfiltered, a morning that asks you to squint before you’ve had your coffee.

It’s a pleasant evening for light layers: a soft jumper or a breathable jacket will be enough if you linger outdoors. Consider a gentle circuit around the block, a pause on the verandah with something warm in your hands, or a quiet errand you’ve been putting off. If you’re up early, plan for quick sunshine—shades and a hat by the door will serve you well—and maybe a short stroll to catch the first gold on the jacarandas before the day gathers speed.
